free_text: Nucleaire -IRSN, 13115 Saint Paul lex Durance, France
9+-1 um thick deposit of Be obtained by evaporation
on a tungsten substrate. Deuterons leave the
beryllium layer with an energy of 946 keV. At this
deuteron energy, any nuclear reaction on tungsten (in
particular neutron producing ones) is strongly
suppressed by the high Coulomb barrier.
LNE-IRSN-MIMAC is a nuclear recoil gas detector
NaI(Tl) gamma-ray detector mounted at 1.5 m
from the target at 90 degree to cross-check the
the current integrator and to evaluate the integrity
of the Be deposit during the experiment.
